title: Odontadenia lutea
intro: Odontadenia lutea is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontadenia lutea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Odontadenia lutea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is part of the genus Odontadenia, which consists of several species native to tropical regions. The Apocynaceae family is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. Odontadenia lutea is further classified into various subcategories within the plant kingdom for more detailed study.
